note: Curated queue for AI 2.0 — ~2.0-odds, auto-settleable markets (BTTS / over 2.5 / over 3.5). Higher reward than the 1.4 model, still data-led. (Corners/cards/shots aren't in the free data feeds, so they can't auto-settle; this run sticks to markets it can verify.)

picks.why: France's firepower vs a leaky Iraq (shipped 4 to Norway) — points to a high-scoring game.
picks.whyRisk: France could ease off once in control; 3.5 is a higher bar than 2.5.
If both are already through it could be a cautious dead rubber.

picks.why: Two strong attacking sides; Norway hit 4 vs Iraq.
picks.whyRisk: A tense must-win can tighten up.

picks.why: Both sides carry goal threat; Austria have scored in their games.
picks.whyRisk: Argentina can shut up shop if qualified.

picks.why: England looked open (4-2 v Croatia); Ghana carry a threat.
picks.whyRisk: England may game-manage if already through.

picks.why: Portugal's quality should produce multiple goals vs Uzbekistan.
picks.whyRisk: Uzbekistan defend deep; could keep it to 2-0.

picks.why: Spain dominate and create high chance volume.
picks.whyRisk: Spain were held 0-0 by Cape Verde once — wastefulness is the risk.

picks.why: Brazil's attack should carry the game past 2.5.
Morocco's quality vs debutants Haiti should mean goals.
picks.whyRisk: Haiti defend deep; Morocco can be wasteful.

picks.why: Two evenly-matched sides that both score and concede.

picks.why: Uruguay should overwhelm debutants Cape Verde.

picks.why: Colombia impressed (3-1 v Uzbekistan) and push games.
